title: Helianthemum estevei
taxonomy: Helianthemum estevei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, commonly known as rockrose. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This plant is a member of the genus Helianthemum, which consists of approximately 120 species of succulent shrubs. The classification of Helianthemum estevei is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperm, and the order Rosales. As a member of the Cistaceae family, Helianthemum estevei is closely related to other species of rockrose.
uses: Helianthemum estevei is a plant species that has been reported to have various traditional medicinal uses. In some regions, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Its leaves and stems are also said to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound care and preventing infections. The plant is also reported to have antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of skin conditions and minor injuries. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Helianthemum estevei for medicinal purposes.

intro: Helianthemum estevei, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Helianthemum estevei include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues.
